signals.title: GBP/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3191 — USD outperformance dominates the G10 scorecard with a +1.206% average gain, while GBP lags at -0.848%. Despite a neutral 0bp nominal carry spread (Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75%), the USD real rate of +1.39% offers a +62bp restrictive premium over GBP's +0.77%. Strong USD data (CESI +30) vs neutral GBP data (+2) reinforces the bilateral divergence. Watch for a break of 1.3150 to accelerate the trend.

signals.title: USD/JPY — BoJ Hawkish Shift + Vol Premium
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.28 — BoJ Deputy Governor Himino warns that yen weakness carries a 'bigger inflation punch' and signals further hikes, contradicting the crowded COT LF net short of -115,036. Options market is pricing significant tail risk with a +4.8% VRP (IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.4%) and a -1.08 25d RR put skew. Intervention risk is acute above 161.80 as hawkish BoJ rhetoric meets extreme spec positioning.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs Retail Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5740 — Retail sentiment is at a 93% long extreme, directly fading the institutional COT LF net short of -22,354. While RBNZ holds at 2.25% with a hawkish bias, the -0.83% real rate remains the most accommodative in G10, fueling the 150bp USD carry advantage. Retail capitulation risk is high if the pair fails to hold the April lows; institutional short-covering requires a hawkish RBNZ surprise on July 8.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Vol Complacency
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4148 — The pair's correlation with WTI Oil has collapsed to -0.465 (z=-1.89σ), indicating that CAD is decoupling from energy prices to trade purely on the 138bp OIS carry deficit vs USD. Unlike other majors, USD/CAD options show relative complacency with a neutral +0.25 25d RR despite retail being 95% short. The carry-driven bid remains intact while the oil correlation is broken.
